message:
fix(codegen): correct JOIN ON/WHERE skip label target and TableStar column resolution

Two bugs in codegen_join_select:

1. ON/WHERE skip label pointed to the innermost loop's *start* label
   (via next_labels.last()), causing an infinite re-evaluation loop when
   the condition failed — the cursor never advanced. Fix: emit a fresh
   skip_label and resolve it immediately before the innermost Next
   opcode, so failed conditions jump to cursor advancement.

2. Remove the spurious Goto after end_label resolution. The standard
   Init→Goto pattern expects end_label to point past the program body
   so Init falls through to Transaction; the extra Goto created an
   unreachable backward jump to the left-table loop start.

Also fix resolve_join_output_count: TableStar("t") previously returned
the first table's column count regardless of the alias. Now it searches
the table list by alias (or table name if no alias), matching the
correct schema for `t.*` expansion in multi-table JOINs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
